summary: Cross-validated Phase 001/002/003 integration contracts: role is an open-string shared contract, trace aliases HLD file_role, layer is optional for Phase 003, and current context queryMode/handler schema would drop the planned omni hld_lld payload.

title: `file_role` is an open string in Phase 001 but a consumed contract in Phase 002
recommendation: Document role as an open string with required baseline labels plus reserved edge labels; make Phase 002 test equality with Phase 001 output rather than a closed enum.

title: `classifyFileRole()` signature is implied but not pinned in Phase 001's public contract
recommendation: Pin and export `classifyFileRole(filePath, db)` or an explicit structured equivalent before Phase 001/002 tests diverge.

title: Phase 001's omni `hld_lld` integration conflicts with the current `code_graph_context` schema
recommendation: Update QueryMode, ContextResult, handler mode parsing, and serialized JSON together; add a handler-level integration test that parses `queryMode:'omni'` output.

title: `architectural_role` should be an alias of HLD `file_role`, not a separate classifier
recommendation: Keep field names context-specific but document `trace.architectural_role === hld.file_role` for the same resolved file.

title: Phase 003's optional `layer` dependency needs an explicit fallback
recommendation: If Phase 001 is unavailable, omit layer weighting or emit `{source:"unavailable", value:null}`; do not create a second local layer classifier in Phase 003.

title: Parallel development stubs can hide contract drift unless they use the real exported shape
recommendation: Allow only typed test doubles matching the final `classifyFileRole(filePath, db)` contract; do not use a production `unclassified` fallback to claim Phase 002 integration.

title: Test isolation depends on a small shared contract test, not a closed enum
recommendation: After Phase 001 lands, assert `generateHLD(file, db).file_role`, `classifyFileRole(file, db)`, and `traceSymbol(symbol, db).architectural_role` match on the same fixture.
